page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u2i-c1m1.xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by a 2.7 GHz Intel Xeon processor. Running on the KVM hypervisor, this instance provides 4 dedicated physical cores with 1 thread per core, resulting in 4 vCPUs. The hardware configuration includes 4.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 1.0 GB per core. There is no local storage bundled with this instance, and it does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Network connectivity is rated at a baseline bandwidth of 3 Gbps.

The primary tradeoff of the ecs.u2i-c1m1.xlarge is its highly constrained memory capacity relative to its processing power. With only 1.0 GB of RAM per vCPU, memory-intensive applications will face resource exhaustion. However, because the CPU allocation is dedicated rather than shared, the instance avoids the performance variability associated with burstable or multi-tenant CPU credit systems. This makes the hardware profile qualitatively cost-efficient for CPU-bound tasks that require stable processing power but have very low memory footprints.

Given the 1:1 ratio of vCPUs to memory and the lack of local storage, this instance is suited for lightweight, compute-centric workloads. Appropriate use cases include basic web serving, microservices, lightweight batch processing, and simple utility tasks that do not require large memory buffers or local disk caching.

It is configured with 4.0 GB of system memory, establishing a 1.0 GB per core ratio, and does not include local storage or GPU hardware. The instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps. Because the CPU allocation is dedicated, it delivers stable processing performance without the variability of burstable models, though the low memory-to-vCPU ratio presents a significant constraint for memory-heavy applications. This profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for lightweight, CPU-bound tasks such as basic web serving, microservices, and simple batch processing.
